rationale: Both papers are closely aligned in core question and method: each uses a randomized field experiment to identify causal effects of firm social-media actions on real demand/behavior outcomes (Weibo tweets/retweets on TV viewing vs. Facebook likes/boosted posts on offline activity). The cited study’s mechanism focus on firm-initiated communication and causal identification appears directly relevant to the citing paper’s stated contribution and relation-to-prior-work, so this is not clearly an incidental mention.
